name: Service Module — Jettison View
description: When the Service Module is finally jettisoned before re-entry, the crew sees the damage for the first time through the window — an entire panel blown away, wiring hanging in the vacuum, the oxygen tank bay a ruin.
atmosphericDetails: The Service Module drifts away slowly, rotating in the sunlight, and the missing panel is immediately visible — a six-foot section of the hull simply gone
Internal components hang in the vacuum, frozen and crystallized, trailing wiring like the roots of a pulled tooth
Lovell photographs it through the window because Houston has asked him to and because he cannot look away
The sight confirms, finally and completely, that the explosion was catastrophic — not a sensor failure, not a misread — real and total
Fred Haise says 'there's one whole side of the spacecraft missing' and then is quiet for a long time
